title: Fridge Tetris
one_liner: You are a leftover container fighting for survival in a fridge that keeps filling up with new groceries.
elevator_pitch: Every Tuesday, your human goes grocery shopping and dumps new items into the fridge. You play as a sentient leftover tupperware that must shuffle, squeeze, and rotate other items to avoid being pushed to the back — the graveyard of forgotten food. Survive long enough and you might actually get eaten (victory!).
core_mechanic: Drag-to-rearrange items in a grid to keep your container visible in the front row
secondary_mechanics: smell meter that triggers human inspection
infinite_stage_design: Each stage is a new grocery shopping trip — items get weirder and larger (a whole watermelon on stage 10), fridge sections unlock (freezer = slippery chaos), and eventually a second fridge appears requiring split attention.
difficulty_curve: Early stages have 3-4 small items. By stage 5, condiment armies invade the door shelf. By stage 15, the human starts meal prepping and fills 80% of the fridge in one wave.
visual_style: Clean cutaway-view fridge with colorful cartoon food items, each with expressive faces. Tupperware protagonist has googly eyes and a worried expression.
sound_concept: Satisfying squish/slide sounds for rearranging. Dramatic grocery-bag-rustling fanfare for each new wave. A faint 'noooo' when pushed to the back.
monetization_hooks: unlock fancier leftover containers as skins
special 'organic grocery' wave that costs coins to skip
viral_hooks: relatable to anyone who has ever done groceries
creator: oddball

title: Sock Singularity
one_liner: You are a lone sock tumbling through the dryer, desperately trying to reunite with your match before the cycle ends.
elevator_pitch: Inside a spinning dryer, socks, shirts, and rogue receipts tumble in chaotic orbits. You control your sock by tilting and tapping to navigate toward your matching sock while avoiding static-charged imposters that look almost-but-not-quite like your match. Every load of laundry is a new level with more socks that all start looking suspiciously similar.
core_mechanic: Tap to boost your sock's trajectory toward the matching sock in a physics-driven tumbling environment
secondary_mechanics: pattern-matching to identify the true pair
static electricity zones that reverse controls
infinite_stage_design: Each load adds more socks with increasingly subtle pattern differences (stage 1: stripes vs polka dots; stage 20: two slightly different shades of grey). New clothing items join to obstruct (hoodies with dangly strings, inside-out jeans). Boss levels: The Lost Sock Dimension where ALL socks are mismatched.
difficulty_curve: Early cycles are slow with 4 socks and obvious patterns. Speed increases each cycle. By stage 8, socks have near-identical patterns requiring split-second visual discrimination. By stage 12, the dryer door wobbles open briefly giving a single-frame glimpse of your match.
visual_style: Warm tumble-dryer orange interior. Socks are chunky cartoon characters with stripe/dot/argyle patterns. Satisfying tumble physics with slight motion blur on fast items.
sound_concept: Muffled thumping dryer rhythm as background beat. Soft fabric 'whoosh' for sock movement. Triumphant 'snap' when pairs reunite. Sad trombone if the cycle ends and you're still single.
monetization_hooks: premium sock pattern packs (designer socks as collectibles)
'Wrinkle Shield' powerup that slows dryer for 5 seconds
viral_hooks: 'This is literally my life' relatability factor
creator: oddball
